title: Evidence Synthesis Tools: A curated directory of strictly open-source software / tools for Evidence Synthesis.
description: Evidence Synthesis Tools is a curated directory of strictly open-source, non-proprietary software supporting the complete evidence synthesis workflow. Evidence synthesis is the systematic process of identifying, evaluating, and integrating all available evidence relevant to a research question to inform scientific conclusions, policy, and practice. The directory covers major stages including search strategy development, citation retrieval, deduplication, AI-assisted screening, data extraction, risk-of-bias assessment, visualization, statistical synthesis, evidence mapping, bibliometrics, qualitative synthesis, and research workflow automation. Only tools with publicly available source code and recognized open-source licenses are included. Proprietary, closed-source, freemium, or institution-restricted software is strictly excluded. Version 1.0 includes 140 verified open-source tools compiled up to 2026 to support transparency, reproducibility, Open Science, and long-term research software sustainability.
notes: This directory includes only tools that meet strict inclusion criteria: (1) released under a recognized open-source license (MIT, GPL, Apache, etc.), (2) public source code repository (GitHub, GitLab, Bitbucket, or similar), (3) no closed-source components or mandatory commercial dependencies, (4) reusable and adequately documented for extension and community development, and (5) relevance to evidence synthesis, systematic reviews, meta-analysis, or related research workflows. Free but closed-source, freemium, or institution-restricted tools are excluded. Contributions are welcome via GitHub issues or pull requests.
